dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4277 PCS: 10 Years of ICE Funding Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Schmitt (for himself, Mr. Hawley, and Mr. Moreno) introduced the following bill; which was read the first time
Read the second time and placed on the calendar

form.official-title: To make appropriations for U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the 10 Years of ICE Funding Act.

subsection.text: For necessary expenses of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement for operations and support, including for the purchase and lease of up to 3,790 police-type vehicles (of which 2,350 vehicles shall be replacement vehicles only), for overseas vetted units, and for maintenance, minor construction, and minor leasehold improvements at owned and leased facilities, $100,363,620,000.

For necessary expenses of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement for procurement, construction, and improvements, including acquisition of necessary additional real property and facilities, construction and ongoing maintenance, facility improvements, equipment, and related expenses, $5,000,000,000.
Amounts appropriated under subsections (a) and (b) shall remain available until September 30, 2036.

Amounts appropriated under subsections (a) and (b) shall remain available for obligation notwithstanding section 501 of the Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act, 2026, or any other provision of law limiting the period of availability of such amounts.

endorsement.action-desc: Read the second time and placed on the calendar
